tweets.text:
10 practical tips for surviving a long bear market ↓

1️⃣ Don't try to win it back in one trade

Oversized revenge trades are how bear markets finish people off.

2️⃣ Keep enough cash on the side

You don't want to become a forced seller at the worst time.

3️⃣ Don't buy every dip

Some dips just keep dipping, so don't chase the exact bottom.

4️⃣ DCA into quality only

DCA helps with timing, but it won't save you from bad assets.

5️⃣ Cut dead bags

If you wouldn't buy it today, ask why you're still holding it.

6️⃣ Watch liquidity, not just price

A token can look cheap but still be impossible to exit cleanly.

7️⃣ Don't chase random yield

When markets are boring, bad "low-risk" opportunities start looking tempting.

8️⃣ Build your watchlist early

Bear markets show which teams are still shipping when nobody's watching.

9️⃣ Fix your security setup

Revoke approvals, clean wallets, secure seed phrases. Reduce dumb risks.

🔟 Get comfortable with boredom

The bottom usually feels slow, ugly, and obvious only in hindsight.

The goal is to still have capital and patience when the market finally gives you a real opportunity again.
tweets.full_text:
10 practical tips for surviving a long bear market ↓

1️⃣ Don't try to win it back in one trade

Oversized revenge trades are how bear markets finish people off.

2️⃣ Keep enough cash on the side

You don't want to become a forced seller at the worst time.

3️⃣ Don't buy every dip

Some dips just keep dipping, so don't chase the exact bottom.

4️⃣ DCA into quality only

DCA helps with timing, but it won't save you from bad assets.

5️⃣ Cut dead bags

If you wouldn't buy it today, ask why you're still holding it.

6️⃣ Watch liquidity, not just price

A token can look cheap but still be impossible to exit cleanly.

7️⃣ Don't chase random yield

When markets are boring, bad "low-risk" opportunities start looking tempting.

8️⃣ Build your watchlist early

Bear markets show which teams are still shipping when nobody's watching.

9️⃣ Fix your security setup

Revoke approvals, clean wallets, secure seed phrases. Reduce dumb risks.

🔟 Get comfortable with boredom

The bottom usually feels slow, ugly, and obvious only in hindsight.

The goal is to still have capital and patience when the market finally gives you a real opportunity again.

tweets.text:
CZ now has a higher net worth than Bill Gates.

One built the entire software industry from scratch, then spent decades giving his fortune away to fight disease and lift people out of poverty.

The other built a monpoly that extracted billions from retail, pleaded guilty to violating anti-money laundering laws, went to prison and somehow came out richer than ever.

I love our industry as it’s full of brilliant people building things that will genuinely change the world, but it’s just a shame that this is still the kind of person who ends up on top of it.

We shouldn't let that happen. Access should be UNIVERSAL. From the smallest town in Cheyenne Wyoming, to a kid in Kathmandu Nepal.

tweets.text:
What’s a huge disinflationary pressure that no one is factoring in alongside immigration?  

What if this is hitting alongside AI productivity and causes inflation to roll over?

From @mansharamani :

“Last year, 3.6 million babies were born; that’s 700,000 fewer than the 2007 peak. The fertility rate hit another record low: 1.6 children per woman, well below the replacement rate, and government demographers believe it will stay near 1.6 through 2055.

But let’s consider the demographic ripple this change in birth rate is causing. What about all those babies that weren’t born in 2009? All those missing babies translates into missing adults now. They would have been turning 18 next year. They’re the first cohort of the “missing generation” and they’re absent from college enrollment lists, military recruiting pools, and entry-level job applicants. 

Between 2008 and 2025, roughly 7.4 million fewer Americans were born than would have been at the 2007 rate. That’s 7.4 million fewer workers, taxpayers, consumers, soldiers, and Social Security contributors. They don’t exist and no policy can create them retroactively….

Syracuse University’s chancellor just told faculty the school will run its first budget deficit in yearsbecause it can’t fill its freshman class. The school cut 93 academic programs; 55 of those had zero enrolled students. To attract the ones who do exist, Syracuse offered tuition discounts of up to $200,000 to students who’d already turned them down. Syracuse isn’t alone. Ohio State cut eight majors and merged 20 others. The University of North Texas slashed 70 programs amid a $45 million deficit. The University of Chicago paused doctoral admissions in over a dozen humanities departments. Demographers call it the “enrollment cliff,” a projected 5 to 10 percent decline in freshman class sizes beginning now.”
